title: adonis autumnalis subsp. baetica
intro: Adonis autumnalis subsp. baetica, also known as Autumn Crocus or Striped Beauty, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe, characterized by its striking, striped flowers and ornamental value. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid compounds and other bioactive substances. The medicinal properties of Adonis autumnalis subsp. baetica are believed to offer various health benefits, including pain relief, wound healing, and protection against infections. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion, and in some cases, it is used topically in ointments and creams for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
